name: Finger Suspicious Invocation
notes:
This rule detects suspicious aged finger.exe tool execution often used in malware attacks nowadays

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), omkar72, oscd.community
SIGMA Title: Finger.exe Suspicious Invocation
SIGMA Description: Detects suspicious aged finger.exe tool execution often used in malware attacks nowadays


Confidence: HIGH

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of finger.exe
TD_list: APPLY Finger Suspicious Invocation on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of finger.exe
RED_list: Event description: Detects suspicious aged finger.exe tool execution often used in malware attacks nowadays
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
